- Target interpretation: Primary-earner substitution-response elasticity for earnings decile 8 in a U.S. microsimulation model

runs.interpretation: Primary-earner substitution-response elasticity for earnings decile 8 in a U.S. microsimulation model
runs.reasoningSummary: Labor supply elasticities for primary earners (often married men) at upper-middle incomes (decile 8) are typically positive but moderate, around 0.1-0.5, higher than for low earners due to less extensive margin constraints and more extensive hours variation; upper tail allows for larger responses."

runs.reasoningSummary: Labor supply elasticities for primary earners (often married men) in upper-middle deciles like 8 are moderately positive, higher than for low earners but below 1; upper deciles face flatter tax schedules, yet substitution response remains in 0.1-0.8 range from recall of empirical meta-analyses on married male elasticities.
